articles.title: What Gaia Eyes does
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes helps you notice how environmental signals, optional Health data, and your own logs may line up over time.

body_sections.title: What it is
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes is a pattern-finding tool. It gives you a calmer read on the day by comparing current conditions with the personal context you choose to share.
It looks across space and Earth signals, local conditions, optional Health data, and the feedback you log in the app so repeated overlap becomes easier to notice.
bullets: Symptoms, check-ins, and notes you log

body_sections.title: What it is not
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes is about patterns, not certainties. It does not diagnose, prove a cause, or replace clinical care.
The goal is to help you notice timing, context, and repeated themes without pretending the app can know everything.

articles.title: How Gaia Eyes uses environmental signals
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes starts with environmental context so it can compare your day with the wider conditions around you.

body_sections.title: What counts as environmental context
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes watches the conditions around you before it tries to say anything personal. That can include space weather, geomagnetic conditions, local weather, temperature swings, pressure, air quality, allergens, and related context.
Those signals help explain what kind of day you are moving through, even before your own body or symptom data is layered in.

body_sections.title: Why the app pays attention to it
paragraphs: Environmental signals can matter because they change the background conditions you are living in. Gaia Eyes uses them as context, not as proof.
If the same outside conditions keep showing up next to the same symptoms or body-state shifts, that repeated overlap becomes more useful to watch.

articles.title: How Gaia Eyes uses optional Health data
articles.summary: Optional Health data gives Gaia Eyes better body-state context, but the app can still work without it.

body_sections.title: Why it is optional
paragraphs: Apple Health is optional in Gaia Eyes. You can use the app without connecting Health at all, and you can still log symptoms, read EarthScope, and follow environmental context.
When you do allow Health access, Gaia Eyes can add sleep, heart-rate, recovery, activity, and related body-state signals to the picture.

body_sections.title: You stay in control
paragraphs: You decide whether to connect Health data and which domains to allow. Gaia Eyes only reads the domains you grant and the app stays explicit that the data is optional.
Connecting Health data does not turn the app into a diagnosis tool. It adds personal context so patterns can become more useful over time.

links.label: Read why Apple Health is optional
See what Health data is used for

articles.title: Patterns, not certainties
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes is designed to help you notice repeated overlap, not to promise exact answers.

body_sections.title: Why Gaia uses careful language
paragraphs: Human days are messy. Sleep debt, weather, stress, travel, recovery, hormones, and timing can all overlap. That is why Gaia Eyes uses language like may, could, and appears to matter.
When the app says something looks important, it is highlighting a useful pattern candidate, not making a guarantee.

body_sections.title: How to use that framing
paragraphs: Use Gaia Eyes to notice timing, ask better questions, and compare your own experience against the day more clearly.
Treat it as observational support. If something feels urgent, severe, or medically important, use professional medical care instead.

articles.title: Scientific and mystical modes
articles.summary: Scientific and mystical modes change the presentation and guide voice, not the underlying data.

body_sections.title: Same data, different presentation
paragraphs: The signal layer does not change when you switch modes. Gaia Eyes still watches the same underlying conditions and the same personal context you choose to share.
What changes is the presentation style: more clinical and direct on one side, more symbolic and reflective on the other.

body_sections.title: Guide and tone basics
paragraphs: Your guide and tone settings shape how Gaia explains the day. They do not change what the app believes is true.
That split is intentional so the app can feel human without drifting away from signal truth.

articles.title: Why Apple Health is optional
articles.summary: You do not need Apple Health connected to use Gaia Eyes.

body_sections.title: What still works without it
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes still shows EarthScope, local conditions, drivers, and anything you log yourself even if you never connect Apple Health.
Optional Health data mostly helps the app add body-state context such as sleep, recovery, heart-rate, activity, and related timing.

body_sections.title: Why the app keeps it optional
paragraphs: Health access is personal. Gaia Eyes keeps it optional so you can decide how much context you want to share.
User stays in control. You can connect later, disconnect later, or only allow selected domains.

links.label: See what Health data is used for

articles.title: Why sleep may not appear immediately
articles.summary: Sleep often has more than one handoff, so it can take time before the full night shows up in Gaia Eyes.

body_sections.title: Why there can be a delay
paragraphs: A full night of sleep may need to pass through your wearable, the wearable app, Apple Health, and then Gaia Eyes. Any lag in that chain can make sleep appear later than expected.
Some devices also finalize stages or totals after you unlock your phone, open the vendor app, or wait for background sync to catch up.

body_sections.title: What to check first
paragraphs: Make sure the sleep session is already visible in Apple Health. If it is not there yet, Gaia Eyes cannot read it yet.
If it is visible in Health but not yet in Gaia Eyes, give the app a little time or run a manual Health refresh from Settings.

links.label: Read how background Health sync works

articles.title: How background Health sync works
articles.summary: After you grant access, Gaia Eyes listens for new Health updates and also runs catch-up imports when needed.

body_sections.title: Normal sync flow
paragraphs: When permissions are granted, Gaia Eyes can watch for new Health data in the background and request refreshes after uploads.
That means many updates arrive without you needing to do anything, but timing still depends on what Apple Health already has available.

body_sections.title: Manual catch-up
paragraphs: If you want Gaia Eyes to catch up on recent history faster, use the manual sync option in Settings. The launch build also includes a last-30-days sync for a stronger starting baseline.
Background sync is useful, but manual catch-up is still the fastest way to fill gaps after a new install or a permissions change.

articles.title: What to do if your wearable app has not synced
articles.summary: If the wearable app has not handed data to Apple Health yet, Gaia Eyes has nothing new to import.

body_sections.title: Where the delay usually starts
paragraphs: Many sync issues start before Gaia Eyes. If the vendor app has not uploaded, processed, or shared the latest session with Apple Health yet, the delay is upstream.
This is common after Bluetooth reconnects, overnight sleep sessions, travel, low battery, or when the wearable app needs to open once before it finishes syncing.

body_sections.title: Best next steps
paragraphs: Open the wearable app, confirm the data appears there, then confirm it reaches Apple Health. Once Apple Health has the data, Gaia Eyes can work with it.
If the data is already in Apple Health and still not showing after a reasonable delay, use a manual sync and contact support if the issue keeps repeating.

articles.title: Location permission and local weather context
articles.summary: Location access helps Gaia Eyes tune local weather and air context to where you actually are.

body_sections.title: Why location matters
paragraphs: Local weather, pressure, air quality, and temperature context are only useful when they are close to your actual location or the ZIP you choose.
Gaia Eyes supports live GPS or a fixed ZIP so you can decide whether you want travel-aware context or a stable local baseline.

body_sections.title: You can still stay in control
paragraphs: If you do not want live location on, you can often use a ZIP instead. That keeps local context available without requiring GPS all the time.
If neither is set, Gaia Eyes can still show broader signal context, but local conditions may be missing or less personal.

articles.title: Why some signals update at different speeds
articles.summary: Different data sources publish on different schedules, so not every part of Gaia Eyes refreshes at the same moment.

body_sections.title: Different sources, different timing
paragraphs: Local conditions, space signals, wearable data, and app-generated summaries do not all come from one stream. Each source has its own cadence, cache behavior, and processing path.
That means one card may already be current while another is still waiting on a provider update, a wearable handoff, or a backend refresh.

body_sections.title: A mismatch is not always a bug
paragraphs: A temporary mismatch often just means the app is showing the newest trustworthy value available for each source.
If something stays stale for too long, then it is worth checking last updated labels, Health sync status, or support.

links.label: Why local, space, and health can differ

articles.title: What last updated means
articles.summary: The last updated label tells you when that specific layer was most recently refreshed, not when every part of the app changed together.

body_sections.title: It is usually per layer
paragraphs: A last updated label usually belongs to one surface, feed, or summary. It is not a promise that every other card, trend, or Health value refreshed at the same time.
This matters because Gaia Eyes combines live context, cached context, and personal signals that can move on different schedules.

body_sections.title: How to read it
paragraphs: Use the timestamp as a clue about freshness for that specific view. If the time looks reasonable for the data source, the app is usually behaving as expected.
If the timestamp is far older than expected, check whether the upstream source, device, or connection may be the reason.

links.label: Read why cached data may appear briefly

articles.title: Why local conditions, space signals, and Health data may not refresh at the same time
articles.summary: Those layers come from different systems, so it is normal for them to settle at different times.

body_sections.title: Three different timing patterns
paragraphs: Space signals often depend on public monitoring feeds and processing windows. Local conditions depend on weather and air providers near your location. Health data depends on Apple Health and whatever device wrote into it.
Gaia Eyes brings those together, but it cannot force them into one universal refresh clock.

body_sections.title: What this means in practice
paragraphs: You may see current space context before local conditions settle. You may see local conditions before a wearable finishes overnight sync. You may see both before a personalized summary recalculates.
That is normal as long as each layer is updating within a reasonable window for its source.

articles.title: Why cached data may appear briefly
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes may briefly show a recent cached value while it asks for a newer one.

body_sections.title: Why the app uses cache at all
paragraphs: Cache helps the app load faster and stay useful when a network request or provider response is still in flight.
It is often better to show a recent value with clear timing than to show nothing while the latest fetch is still settling.

body_sections.title: When to worry
paragraphs: A brief cached state is normal. A value that stays old for too long is the point where troubleshooting makes sense.
If you are on a development build, the diagnostics surface can also help explain whether the app is falling back to cache.

articles.title: Symptoms vs current symptoms
articles.summary: Symptoms are everything you log. Current symptoms are the active, still-relevant symptom picture Gaia Eyes is tracking right now.

body_sections.title: What is the difference
paragraphs: Your symptom log is the full record of what you entered. Current symptoms is the smaller active snapshot Gaia Eyes uses to show what still seems in play right now.
That active snapshot can include state changes such as improving, ongoing, or resolved, plus likely drivers or follow-up prompts when relevant.

body_sections.title: Why Gaia separates them
paragraphs: Without a current layer, the app would treat every old symptom like it is still equally loud. Current symptoms gives Gaia a more realistic read on the present moment.
It also makes follow-ups and driver explanations easier to understand without reopening your full history every time.

articles.title: What drivers mean
articles.summary: Drivers are the signals Gaia Eyes thinks are standing out most in the current picture.

body_sections.title: How to read a driver
paragraphs: A driver is not a diagnosis and it is not a guaranteed cause. It is a signal or condition Gaia Eyes thinks is unusually relevant to what is happening right now.
Some drivers are leading. Others are supporting. Together they help explain why the app is emphasizing one pattern over another.

body_sections.title: Why the list can change
paragraphs: Driver rankings change as the day changes, new data arrives, or your personal context shifts. That is normal.
A driver is a live interpretation layer, not a permanent label.

links.label: Why today's leading signal may change

articles.title: How patterns are found
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes compares repeated overlap between what you log, what your body data shows, and what the day looked like around you.

body_sections.title: The basic loop
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes watches signals, collects the feedback you log, and looks for repeated overlap over time. The more a theme repeats in a meaningful way, the more useful it becomes to watch.
This is why consistency matters. One rough day is a clue. A repeating pattern across similar days is much more useful.

body_sections.title: What a pattern still does not mean
paragraphs: Even a repeated pattern is still observational. It can help you notice timing and ask better questions, but it does not prove a mechanism.
Gaia Eyes is built to surface patterns, not certainties.

articles.title: What EarthScope is
articles.summary: EarthScope is Gaia Eyes' translated snapshot of the day: the current read, the strongest drivers, and the quickest route into deeper layers.

body_sections.title: What EarthScope is for
paragraphs: EarthScope is the fast, human-readable summary layer. It gives you a plain-language read on what may matter right now without making you parse every raw metric first.
It usually combines the current conditions, highlighted drivers, and a short interpretation of the day.

body_sections.title: What it points toward
paragraphs: If EarthScope says something looks important, you can usually open deeper views for drivers, patterns, local conditions, or related context.
Think of it as the front door, not the whole house.

articles.title: Why today's leading signal may change
articles.summary: The strongest signal right now is a live ranking, so it can move as the day evolves.

body_sections.title: Why rankings move
paragraphs: A leading signal can change when new environmental data arrives, when Health data catches up, or when your own logs make a different pattern more relevant.
Gaia Eyes would rather update the ranking honestly than pretend the first guess was final.

body_sections.title: How to use it
paragraphs: Treat the leading signal as the app's best current read, not a permanent conclusion.
If the same signal keeps returning across similar days, that is often more informative than whether it stayed number one every hour.

articles.title: Free vs Plus
articles.summary: Free keeps the core experience available. Plus adds richer personalization and more useful day-to-day context.

body_sections.title: What Free is for
paragraphs: Free is there so you can understand the product and start using the core experience without committing right away.
It is the lower-friction way to see whether Gaia Eyes feels useful to you.

body_sections.title: What Plus adds
paragraphs: Plus is meant for people who want stronger current-state personalization and better everyday context.
The exact entitlement mix can change over time, but the high-level idea is more personal signal interpretation and richer day-to-day guidance.

articles.title: Restore purchases
articles.summary: If paid access looks missing, first sign in with the same account and refresh your membership status.

body_sections.title: Start with the account itself
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes matches access to the account used during checkout. If access looks missing, make sure you are signed in with that same email or account first.
After that, refresh membership status so the app can fetch your latest entitlements.

body_sections.title: When to contact support
paragraphs: If the right account is signed in and access still looks wrong after a refresh, contact billing help with the email used at checkout and a short description of what looks off.
That makes it much easier to track down the mismatch quickly.

articles.title: How subscriptions work
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes uses account-based billing, so your access follows the account you used at checkout.

body_sections.title: Access follows the account
paragraphs: Your plan is tied to the account used for checkout, not only to one device. That is why signing in with the correct account matters when you switch devices or reinstall the app.
The app checks your current entitlements and uses that result to decide which plan is active.

body_sections.title: Changes and renewals
paragraphs: Upgrade, renewal, and billing changes depend on the billing flow you used. Gaia Eyes shows your current plan and gives you a billing-management path when one is available.
If something looks wrong, start with a refresh and then contact billing help if needed.

articles.title: Managing subscriptions on Apple devices
articles.summary: On launch, Gaia Eyes billing may open a secure web billing flow rather than using the App Store subscription screen.

body_sections.title: Where to manage your plan
paragraphs: If Gaia Eyes gives you a Manage Billing button, use that first. In the current launch setup, billing changes may happen through the secure billing portal instead of the App Store subscriptions page.
That means the right management path depends on how the plan was originally purchased.

body_sections.title: If you are not sure
paragraphs: Open Account and Membership and look for the plan status or billing-management link there.
If you still are not sure where the subscription lives, billing help can confirm the right path.

articles.title: Offer codes and promo basics
articles.summary: If promo or offer codes are available for your plan, they usually appear during the secure checkout flow.

body_sections.title: Where to look
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes can support promotion codes in the checkout flow when that billing path is enabled for the plan you are using.
If a promotion applies, it should show up in checkout rather than needing a separate manual unlock in the app.

body_sections.title: If a code does not work
paragraphs: Double-check the code, the plan, and whether the offer is still active. Some offers only apply to certain plans or certain windows.
If a valid-looking code still fails, contact billing help and include the code plus a screenshot if you can.

articles.title: What data stays on device vs backend
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes uses a mix of on-device state, synced account data, and backend-generated context depending on the feature.

body_sections.title: What can stay local
paragraphs: Some app state, preferences, recent cache, and temporary UI context can live on device so the app loads quickly and keeps your setup stable.
That local state helps with usability. It is not the same thing as the personalized account data the app may sync when features need it.

body_sections.title: What may use backend systems
paragraphs: Account-linked features, plan status, synced signals, and some generated summaries can rely on backend services so the experience stays consistent across sessions and devices.
Gaia Eyes aims to be transparent about that split and avoids pretending every feature is purely local when it is not.

articles.title: What Health data is used for
articles.summary: Health data is used to add body-state context, not to make diagnoses.

body_sections.title: What it helps with
paragraphs: Optional Health data can help Gaia Eyes understand sleep, recovery, activity, and related body-state timing more clearly.
That makes personalized patterns easier to notice because the app can compare environmental conditions with what your body data was doing around the same time.

body_sections.title: What it does not do
paragraphs: Health data does not turn Gaia Eyes into a clinical tool. The app uses it as context, not as diagnosis, treatment, or medical proof.
No diagnosis and no medical advice remain core product rules.

articles.title: User control over permissions
articles.summary: You choose what Gaia Eyes can access and you can revisit those choices later.

body_sections.title: What you control
paragraphs: You stay in control of Health permissions, notification choices, and local-context settings. Gaia Eyes is designed so those decisions are explicit and revisitable.
You can grant access later, turn categories off later, or keep parts of the app disconnected if that fits your comfort level better.

body_sections.title: Why that matters
paragraphs: Trust matters more than squeezing every possible signal out of you. Gaia Eyes is meant to be useful without forcing an all-or-nothing setup.
That is why optional Health data and permission controls are treated as product principles, not just settings.

articles.title: No diagnosis and no medical advice
articles.summary: Gaia Eyes is not a diagnostic tool and its explanations are not medical advice.

body_sections.title: The limit is intentional
paragraphs: Gaia Eyes is designed to help you observe patterns and context. It is not designed to diagnose illness, confirm causes, or tell you what treatment to use.
That limit stays in place whether the app is reading only environmental data or combining it with optional Health data and personal logs.

body_sections.title: When to seek care
paragraphs: If something feels urgent, severe, or medically important, use a licensed medical professional or emergency care as appropriate.
Gaia Eyes can support awareness. It cannot replace medical judgment.

articles.title: Report a bug
articles.summary: The fastest bug reports include what you expected, what happened instead, and which screen you were on.

body_sections.title: What helps most
paragraphs: Tell us what you expected, what happened instead, and how often it happens. A screenshot and the exact screen name are both helpful.
If the problem looks tied to Health sync, billing, or a stale timestamp, say that clearly in the message subject.

articles.title: Send feedback
articles.summary: Product feedback is welcome, especially when you can say what felt useful, confusing, or missing.

body_sections.title: What makes feedback useful
paragraphs: The most helpful feedback is specific. Tell us which part of the app you were using, what felt unclear, and what would have made the experience easier to trust or use.
If you are suggesting a new feature, it also helps to explain the problem you were trying to solve.

articles.title: Need help with billing?
articles.summary: Billing help is easiest when you send the email used at checkout plus a short description of the issue.

body_sections.title: What to include
paragraphs: Send the email used during checkout, the plan you expected to have, and what looks wrong right now.
If you already tried refreshing your account status or opening the billing portal, mention that too.

articles.title: Health sync help
articles.summary: If Health data looks delayed or missing, start by checking where the sync chain stopped.

body_sections.title: What to say in your message
paragraphs: Tell us which data is missing, whether it already appears in Apple Health, and whether the issue is repeatable or only happened once.
That makes it much easier to tell the difference between a device delay, a Health permission issue, and an app-side problem.
